steps.title: Find the month four balance
steps.content:
The balance after $4$ months is the original $950$ dollars plus $0.8\%$ of that original balance.
$$950(1.008)=957.60$$

steps.title: Count the growth periods
steps.content:
After month $4$, the balance grows by $0.2\%$ every $2$ months. The number of two-month periods from month $4$ to month $x$ is
$$\frac{x-4}{2}=\frac{x}{2}-\frac{4}{2}$$

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content:
Use initial amount $957.60$, growth factor $1.002$, and the two-month period count as the exponent.
$$B(x)=957.60(1.002)^{\frac{x}{2}-\frac{4}{2}}$$
The answer is C.
